title: Level 26
wikitext:
{{Blurbackground|file=Level26.jpg}} {{DISPLAYTITLE:'''Level 26:''' ''Otherworldly'' }} {{Image Banner|caption=''The otherworldly is beautiful.
I feel natural in the irreal.''|image=Level26.jpg}}
{{Class 3}} '''Level 26''' is the 27th level of the Backrooms. {{Blurimage|width=360px|align=right|file=Level26.jpg|hovertext=Wrong and right are true nonetheless.|caption=A hallway within Level 26.}} == Description == '''Level 26''' manifests as an expansive conglomeration of apartments spread across an indeterminate distance. The architechture of the level primarily consists of narrow, winding hallways linking various interconnected chambers. The geometry of '''Level 26''' is commonly described to be disorderly and confusing, often carrying redundant passageways, dead-ends, and nonsensical structures. The level is affected significantly by [[Phenomenon 14]] in it's indoor sections, resulting in extreme spatial distortion in most areas. The walls of the level are generally made from brick and are layered over with yellow plaster appearing to maintain relatively high quality, although they often have small scratches. The carpet of the level is typically green or beige in color and is described to similarly be pristine in most cases, although in others it may show signs of wear. The fluorescent lights emit a quiet hum buzz constant throughout the entire level, although in certain areas the lighting may be faulty. '''Level 26''' has a system of air vents which noticibly cools down the level but not to an uncomfortable degree. The sizes of the hallways in the level vary significantly; there are examples of halls under a meter in width while others extend to widths of over thirty feet. The level is believed to be based on a singular floor; stairwells that appear in the level will cause one to simply loop into other areas of the floor rather than bringing one to a new floor. Located alongside the extensive hallways of the level, one may find various rooms resembling those that would be found in a standard apartment. Said rooms typically have a bed, desk or small table, and minor decorations such as paintings or potted plants. Although a majority of rooms within the level look alike, there exist many examples of rooms which differ completely, some devoid of decorations and objects, while others are overflowing with disordered clusters of them. Furniture within '''Level 26''' may exhibit anomalous properties; objects may be found floating onto ceilings or hovering in the air without legs. Interacting with these anomalous objects will swiftly remove their properties, which may in certain cases cause harm if an object falls onto the one investigating them. Bookshelves are common within the level, but the books contained within typically contain useless or nonsensical titles or text. Drawers and cabinets, may, albeit rarely, contain resources including packaged food, bottled water and [[Object 1|Almond Water]], and various tin cans. Despite usually being slightly stale, most food found within '''Level 26''' can be safely consumed, although caution is still advised. {{Blurimage|width=360px|align=left|file=Level-26-1.png|hovertext=The fog replied once more.|caption=An image of '''Level 26''''s exterior.}} === The Exterior === Although a large amount of windows within '''Level 26''' are manifestations of [[Entity 2]], certain windows open up into what is known as the "exterior" of the level. The exterior of the level manifests as an expansive, foggy forest expanding endlessly. The air of the exterior is windy and much colder than the interior apartments, so preparation is advised if one must travel through it for extended periods of time. Unlike the interior area, the geometry of the exterior is much more stable and thus is much easier to navigate around, especially with the more sparse environment. However, due to the high instability of the interior, attempting to exit the exterior is much more difficult. If one is attempting to enter the exterior, one should maintain close proximity to a safe window allowing secure reentry. The soil of the exterior is dry and sterile, so how the trees and grass are able to grow in such an environment is unknown. One can hear the noise of birds occasionally, seeming to originate somewhere within the fog, although no such birds or any other animal life have been visibly spotted within the level. The exterior of the level is devoid of entities and thus is somewhat more safe than the interior. Why entities do not appear in the exterior is unknown, as there are no major obstructions that could prevent them from entering. ----
Recovered Note:
'''''Solidarity'''''

Amongst the dissonant locations composing the wake, the otherworldly is indistinct; though some may see it as mundane, indifferent from the levels preceding itself and the many others who follow, it itself has a unique identity nevertheless. From what aspects does this arise from? The reason lies within the simplicity, a landscape since unchanged despite the flow of time eroding places elsewhere, distorting them and their recognition. From where such resilience originates from, or why it needs it, or what prompted it such, is an enigma; something lacking explaination neither needing nor desiring to be explained. The otherworldly remains a pillar in time; Never to decay, yet not seeking for more.

---- == Entities == '''Level 26''' has a high concentration of [[Entity 2|Windows]] which commonly dot the various rooms of the level. [[Entity 3|Smilers]] can be found in darker sections of the level, although with a lower frequency. The level is also host to an entity unique to the level known as '''Abstract Art''', appearing the most common. === Abstract Art === {{Blurimage|width=360px|align=right|file=The End Year Book.png|hovertext=Concealment.|caption=An instance of '''Abstract Art''' resembling a book.}} '''Abstract Art''' resemble small, usually decorative objects, typically manifesting as potted plants, books, paintings, and lamps. Instances of the entity are capable of basic locomotion by propelling themselves, usually slowly, across floors, although in a majority of situations they remain dormant. '''Abstract Art''' are capable of both "walking" at slow speeds towards targets and "dashing" by sliding quickly in bursts. The skin of the entity is capable of modifying its own adhesive abilities, allowing instances to latch onto walls for extended periods of time. The texture of the skin, despite looking visually like whatever object the entity appears to resemble, is described to feel akin to fine sandpaper. In most situations, '''Abstract Art''' are found dormant and oftentimes cannot be distinguished from normal objects. '''Abstract Art''' have intellectual capabilities similar to that of turtles or tortoises and thus are not significant hazards, although they are sentient and will react to external stimuli. The entities seem to be incapable of communication, although conglomerations of them seem to form often, obstructing thinner hallways in the level. The frequency in which instances of the entity appear is unknown; estimates state that there are usually one every few hallways, although in some cases there can be far more. == Colonies == Currently, no colonies or outposts exist within '''Level 26''', most likely due to the level's lack of significant resources, although setting up colonies is still technically possible though difficult to the constant shifting of the level's layout. == Thresholds == ==== Entrances: ==== * '''Level 26''' is most commonly entered through stairways within [[The Attic]] and [[The Basement]], though similar stairways have been noted to appear on other levels. ==== Exits: ==== * Certain stairways may lead one to [[The Attic]], [[Level 26.1]], and [[Level 27]], seemingly appearing at random. * Areas in which the level's geometry becomes increasingly haphazard, even for it's standards, may transition into [[Level 193]]. * Finally, out-of-place wooden doors in this level may lead to [[Level 25]], [[Level 31]], and [[Level 35]] when passed through.
